title: What Music & Instruments Is
body:
Music is organised sound that humans use to express emotion, build community, and carry meaning across time. At the physical level, it is vibration, frequency, rhythm, and resonance. At the human level, it is memory, identity, and feeling turned into structure.

Instruments are the tools that make music possible. Strings, winds, brass, percussion, keyboards, and electronic devices each create sound in different ways, which is why they produce different timbres and enable different styles of music. The history of instruments is also the history of materials, craftsmanship, and invention.

No single musical system is universal. Tuning, rhythm, and scale vary across cultures, and that diversity is part of what makes music such a rich subject. Music is not just a language of sound. It is a map of human culture.
keyPoints: Music is organised sound with emotional and social meaning
Instruments shape the sounds music can make
Different cultures use different tuning and rhythm systems
Instrument history is part of human technological history
Music carries identity and memory across generations
Sound physics and culture both matter
furtherReading: 'This Is Your Brain on Music' by Daniel J. Levitin
'The Rest Is Noise' by Alex Ross
